text: Science has a clearer story about publication bias than social media does. Publication bias occurs when studies with positive results are more likely to be published, skewing our understanding. Imagine relying on incomplete data to make health decisions. Frightening, right? Studies show that more than 50% of negative results never see the light of day. This means your health decisions could be based on a distorted view of reality. Stay informed. Question the sources. Like and follow for more insights on science.
